issues.title: MCP tool callback integration not working
issues.body:
**Bug description**
After setting `spring.ai.mcp.client.toolcallback.enabled` to `true` the MCP tool callback integration doesn't seem to be active.
The configured MCP server is detected at startup but simple requests made by the auto-configured ChatClient to OpenAI models contain no tool definitions.
Same MCP server with same configuration json is working fine in Claude Desktop client.

**Environment**
Spring AI 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT @ March 26, 2025, 7:00 PM PDT
Java 21

comments.body:
Sorry had wrong indications from gpt-4o :sweat_smile:
I see from reference doc that you have to explicitly provide tool callbacks and/or tool callback providers to the chat client.
Closing the issue.
There is simply no such configuration option as "spring.ai.mcp.client.toolcallback.enabled". It's really frustrating.

> There is simply no such configuration option as "spring.ai.mcp.client.toolcallback.enabled". It's really frustrating.

Had a quick look at the source code and I think that property isn't mapped by any configuration bean, that's way no metadata are generated for it.
But there's a conditional bean wich is activated only if the afore-mentioned property is set to `true` so that flag is needed in anyway.
